title: Japanese Wagyu Beef Gyudon Recipe
description: A classic Japanese dish made with rich, tender wagyu beef, onions, and a savory soy-based broth. Serve over a bed of steaming hot rice.

ingredients.name: Wagyu Beef (Thinly Sliced)
ingredients.notes: Choose high-quality wagyu beef for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice the onion to help it cook evenly.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality soy sauce for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Sake (Japanese Rice Wine)
ingredients.notes: Add a splash of sake for added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balance the savory flavors with a touch of sugar.

ingredients.name: Mirin (Sweet Japanese Cooking Wine)
ingredients.notes: Use mirin to add a sweet and savory flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Green Onions
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice the green onions for garnish.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate the ginger for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Sesame Oil
ingredients.notes: Use sesame oil for added depth of flavor.

instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add thinly sliced onions and cook until caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add grated ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add sliced wagyu beef and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, sake, sugar, and mirin to the skillet and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Serve the wagyu beef and sauce over a bed of steaming hot rice.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions and serve immediately.
